description:
**Problem.** Process-local state is fragile by design:
- `sessions` Map at `review-state.ts:96` has no eviction ‚Üí slow memory leak in long-running MCP servers.
- Server restart between hop-1 and hop-2 destroys the `reviewId` (in-memory only).
- T-014's disk cache persists only *completed rounds* ‚Äî not in-flight state.

**Non-goal: SQLite.** `cache/session.ts` already has the right primitives (atomic tmp+rename, mode 0o600, schema-versioned, TTL sweep). Extending that is ~10x cheaper than `better-sqlite3`.

---

**Design ‚Äî DECIDED: disk is source of truth; Map becomes a bounded LRU read-cache.**
Not "add eviction to the Map" ‚Äî that keeps the Map authoritative and the disk as a side-channel. The correct design once disk records exist is:
1. `lens_review_complete` reads records from disk by `(reviewId, lensId, attempt)`.
2. Recent reads populate a bounded LRU Map (default 100 entries).
3. Map miss ‚Üí disk read ‚Üí Map populate. Cheap because disk reads are cheap.
4. Map eviction on size cap is fine; disk record is still there. Eviction is not data loss.

This makes restart-between-hops work for free ‚Äî the Map is ephemeral, disk is durable.

**Path scheme ‚Äî FIXED.** Use `tmpdir()/lenses-in-flight/<reviewId>/<lensId>.<attempt>.json`. Match the existing `tmpdir()/lenses-sessions/` convention from `cache/session.ts`. Do NOT use a project-local `.lenses/in-flight/` ‚Äî mixing tmpdir + project-local state confuses users and muddies the cleanup contract.

**Record shape:**
```
{
  taskId: string,                  // sha256(reviewId + ":" + lensId + ":" + attempt)
  reviewId: string,
  lensId: LensId,
  attempt: number,                 // 1-indexed; T-022 max_attempts default is 2
  status: "pending" | "in_flight" | "completed" | "failed" | "expired",
  promptHash: string,
  chunkIndex: number | null,       // nullable day 1 for T-025 chunking; avoids a second migration
  startedAt: string,               // ISO 8601
  completedAt: string | null,
  expiresAt: string,               // ISO 8601; ownership: T-022 server-side at hop-1 from T-023's lensTimeout
  errorCode: LensErrorCode | null  // typed enum ‚Äî see below
}
```

**Typed error enum ‚Äî DEFINED.** Codex's critique (#6) was right: control-plane codes matter more in this architecture than model-flavored codes, because the server doesn't call LLMs.

```
type LensErrorCode =
  | "PARSE_FAILURE"       // finding-level .strict() failed; lens surfaced in parseErrors[]
  | "DUPLICATE_COMPLETE"  // second lens_review_complete for same (reviewId, lensId, attempt)
  | "REVIEW_EXPIRED"      // resubmission past expiresAt
  | "REVIEW_CANCELLED"    // caller cancelled round
  | "PARTIAL_RESULTS"     // some lenses timed out; verdict derived from survivors
  | "MERGE_CONFLICT"      // two completions with incompatible state
  | "CONFIG_MISMATCH"     // mergerConfig changed mid-round
  | "AGENT_TIMEOUT"       // lens exceeded expiresAt without completing
  | "UNKNOWN_ERROR";
```
Each code paired with a user-facing actionable message (follow codex-bridge's `src/utils/errors.ts` pattern).

**Files touched.**
- `src/cache/in-flight.ts` ‚Äî NEW. Mirrors `src/cache/session.ts` primitives (atomic tmp+rename, 0o600, schema versioning, TTL sweep).
- `src/tools/start.ts` ‚Äî write `status: "pending"` record per activated lens on hop-1.
- `src/tools/complete.ts` ‚Äî read records on hop-2; idempotent on `(reviewId, lensId, attempt)`; update `status`/`completedAt`/`errorCode`. Preserve the outside-outer-try/catch cache-isolation at line 400-401.
- `src/tools/get-prompt.ts` (NEW in T-022) ‚Äî reads `promptHash` from disk records.
- `src/review-state.ts` ‚Äî Map becomes bounded LRU read-cache; eviction is no-op on data (disk has it).
- `src/schema/error-code.ts` ‚Äî NEW. Exports `LensErrorCode` union.
- Tests: restart-mid-review recovery; `(reviewId, lensId, attempt)` idempotence; typed-enum exhaustiveness (bidirectional `Record<LensErrorCode, true>` map, same pattern as `LensId` in T-021's public-api.test.ts).

**Preserve RULES.md ¬ß4.** Structural discipline: persistence failure must never flip `isError: true`. Calls run outside the outer try/catch in `complete.ts` (same pattern as `persistRoundBestEffort` at line 400-401).

**Acceptance.**
- `reviewId` generated by `lens_review_start` is recoverable by `lens_review_complete` after an MCP-server restart. Test uses an in-process server restart harness.
- `sessions` Map is a bounded LRU read-cache with disk as source of truth. Test asserts: populate cache to cap, evict LRU entry, subsequent read finds it on disk.
- Every record has `chunkIndex?: number | null` ‚Äî pinned by Zod schema.
- `LensErrorCode` enum is exhaustively covered (compile-time and runtime).
- RULES.md ¬ß4 structurally preserved ‚Äî persistence failure never flips `isError: true`. Test: mock `fs.writeFile` to throw; assert review still succeeds.

**Blocked by T-022** ‚Äî T-022 decides `expiresAt` ownership and the `(reviewId, lensId, attempt)` idempotence key shape that this ticket persists.
title: Disk persistence for in-flight review state (extend cache/session.ts JSON pattern)
